name: Event Sources
description: Four SourceProtocol implementations for ingesting events into Chronicler. (1) webhook.py — aiohttp HTTP POST receiver accepting JSON events, validates against Event schema. (2) otlp.py — OTLP/HTTP JSON receiver at /v1/traces, flattens ExportTraceServiceRequest into individual Events. (3) file.py — JSONL file tailer using asyncio polling with aiofiles for non-blocking reads. (4) sentinel.py — Sentinel incident lifecycle webhook with sentinel-prefixed event_kind. All sources implement start/stop/subscribe, catch exceptions internally, log and continue. Covers AC6 (multiple source types), AC13 (error resilience).

types.name: Event

validators.error_message: Must be ISO 8601 UTC (ending in Z)

types.description: Immutable event envelope — imported from schemas component. Referenced here for type completeness.

types.name: EventKind

validators.error_message: Must be dot-delimited lowercase segments with at least two segments

types.description: Dot-delimited event kind string (e.g. 'webhook.push', 'sentinel.incident.created'). Minimum two segments.

types.name: EventCallback
types.description: Async callable that receives an Event. Type signature: Callable[[Event], Awaitable[None]].

types.name: SourceProtocol

fields.name: start
fields.description: Initialize resources and begin accepting/polling events

fields.name: subscribe
fields.description: Register async event handler callback, synchronous registration

types.description: Runtime-checkable typing.Protocol defining the source interface. All sources must implement start(), stop(), and subscribe(). Decorated with @runtime_checkable.

types.name: BindAddress

validators.error_message: Must be a valid IPv4 address, localhost, or IPv6 shorthand

types.description: Network bind configuration for HTTP-based sources.

types.name: WebhookSourceConfig

fields.name: client_max_size_bytes
fields.description: Maximum request body size in bytes

validators.error_message: Max body size must be between 1KB and 10MB

fields.name: source_name
fields.description: Source identifier stamped on emitted events

types.description: Configuration for the webhook HTTP POST source.

types.name: OtlpSourceConfig

fields.name: client_max_size_bytes
fields.description: Maximum request body size in bytes

validators.error_message: Max body size must be between 1KB and 50MB

fields.name: source_name
fields.description: Source identifier stamped on emitted events

types.description: Configuration for the OTLP/HTTP JSON trace receiver source.

types.name: FileSourceConfig

fields.name: path
fields.description: Path to the JSONL file to tail

validators.error_message: Poll interval must be between 0.1 and 300 seconds

fields.name: source_name
fields.description: Source identifier stamped on emitted events

types.description: Configuration for the JSONL file tailer source.

types.name: FilePath

validators.error_message: File path must not contain null bytes

types.description: A validated filesystem path string. Must be non-empty and not contain null bytes.

types.name: SentinelSourceConfig

fields.name: client_max_size_bytes
fields.description: Maximum request body size in bytes

validators.error_message: Max body size must be between 1KB and 10MB
Event kind prefix must be lowercase alphanumeric with underscores

fields.name: source_name
fields.description: Source identifier stamped on emitted events

types.description: Configuration for the Sentinel incident lifecycle webhook source. Reuses HTTP transport from webhook, adds Sentinel-specific parsing.

types.name: OtlpSpan

fields.name: start_time_unix_nano
fields.description: Span start time in Unix nanoseconds

fields.name: end_time_unix_nano
fields.description: Span end time in Unix nanoseconds

types.description: Intermediate representation of a single OTLP span extracted from ExportTraceServiceRequest, before conversion to Event.

types.name: HttpResponse
types.description: Simplified HTTP response representation for webhook/OTLP handler return values.

types.name: SourceState
types.description: Lifecycle state of a source instance.

types.name: FilePosition

fields.name: offset
fields.description: Byte offset of last read position

fields.name: line_buffer
fields.description: Partial line buffer for incomplete reads

types.description: Tracks current read position in a tailed file for resumption and truncation detection.

functions.name: emit
functions.description: Shared async utility in base.py. Iterates all registered subscriber callbacks and invokes each with the given Event. Each callback is wrapped in try/except Exception so one failing subscriber does not block others. Errors are logged with structured context (event_id, subscriber index).

inputs.name: event
inputs.description: The event to fan out to all subscribers

preconditions: event is a valid Event instance
postconditions: Every subscriber in the list has been called exactly once with the event
Exceptions from individual subscribers are caught, logged, and do not propagate
All subscribers are called regardless of failures in earlier subscribers

structured_side_effects.description: Logs errors for any subscriber callback that raises an exception

functions.name: WebhookSource.subscribe
functions.description: Register an async event handler callback. Synchronous operation — appends callback to internal subscriber list. May be called before or after start(). No unsubscribe mechanism.
preconditions: callback is a non-null async callable
postconditions: callback is appended to internal subscriber list
callback will receive all subsequent events emitted by this source

structured_side_effects.description: Appends callback to internal subscriber list

functions.name: WebhookSource.start
functions.description: Initialize aiohttp AppRunner and TCPSite, bind to configured host:port, begin accepting HTTP POST requests. Registers a single POST endpoint that validates JSON body against Event schema via model_validate_json(), returns 200 on success, 400 on validation failure, 415 on wrong Content-Type. Sets client_max_size on the aiohttp app.

error_cases.name: bind_failed
error_cases.condition: Configured host:port is unavailable or in use

preconditions: Source is in CREATED state (not already started)
postconditions: HTTP server is listening on configured bind address and port
Source state transitions to RUNNING
Incoming valid POST requests will be parsed and emitted to subscribers

structured_side_effects.description: Creates aiohttp AppRunner and TCPSite listening for HTTP connections
Transitions source state from CREATED to RUNNING
Logs successful bind address and port

functions.name: WebhookSource.stop
functions.description: Gracefully shut down the aiohttp HTTP server. Cleans up AppRunner and TCPSite. Idempotent — safe to call multiple times or on a source that was never started.
postconditions: HTTP server is no longer accepting connections
All aiohttp resources (AppRunner, TCPSite) are cleaned up
Source state transitions to STOPPED

structured_side_effects.description: Shuts down aiohttp AppRunner and TCPSite
Transitions source state to STOPPED
Logs shutdown

functions.name: WebhookSource._handle_post
functions.description: Internal HTTP POST handler. Validates Content-Type is application/json, reads body, parses via Event.model_validate_json(), emits to subscribers via _emit, returns HttpResponse. All exceptions caught — invalid JSON or schema returns 400, other errors return 500.

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: JSON does not match Event schema

postconditions: Returns status 200 with event_id in body on success
Returns status 400 with error detail on validation failure
Returns status 415 if Content-Type is not application/json
Returns status 500 on unexpected internal error
Valid events are emitted to all subscribers before response is returned

structured_side_effects.description: Logs validation errors and internal errors with request context

functions.name: OtlpSource.subscribe
functions.description: Register an async event handler callback for OTLP-derived events. Synchronous operation.
preconditions: callback is a non-null async callable
postconditions: callback is appended to internal subscriber list

structured_side_effects.description: Appends callback to internal subscriber list

functions.name: OtlpSource.start
functions.description: Initialize aiohttp AppRunner and TCPSite, bind to configured host:port, begin accepting OTLP/HTTP JSON requests at POST /v1/traces. Sets client_max_size on the aiohttp app.

error_cases.name: bind_failed
error_cases.condition: Configured host:port is unavailable or in use

preconditions: Source is in CREATED state (not already started)
postconditions: HTTP server is listening on configured bind address and port at /v1/traces
Source state transitions to RUNNING

structured_side_effects.description: Creates aiohttp AppRunner and TCPSite at /v1/traces
Transitions source state from CREATED to RUNNING
Logs successful bind

functions.name: OtlpSource.stop
functions.description: Gracefully shut down the OTLP HTTP server. Idempotent.
postconditions: HTTP server is no longer accepting connections
Source state transitions to STOPPED

structured_side_effects.description: Shuts down aiohttp AppRunner and TCPSite
Transitions source state to STOPPED
Logs shutdown

functions.name: OtlpSource._handle_traces
functions.description: Internal POST /v1/traces handler. Parses OTLP JSON ExportTraceServiceRequest, flattens resourceSpans→scopeSpans→spans into individual OtlpSpan structs, converts each to an Event with event_kind 'otlp.trace.{span.name}', emits to subscribers. Returns empty JSON object '{}' per OTLP HTTP spec. All exceptions caught internally.

inputs.name: request_body
inputs.description: Raw HTTP request body containing OTLP JSON

error_cases.name: malformed_otlp
error_cases.condition: JSON does not contain expected resourceSpans structure

postconditions: Returns status 200 with body '{}' on success (per OTLP spec)
Returns status 400 on malformed OTLP JSON
Returns status 415 if Content-Type is not application/json
Each span in the request is converted to an individual Event and emitted
Partial failures (some spans invalid) still process remaining valid spans

structured_side_effects.description: Logs per-span parse errors and unexpected exceptions

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n = total spans across all resourceSpans

functions.name: OtlpSource._flatten_spans
functions.description: Pure function that extracts individual OtlpSpan structs from a parsed OTLP ExportTraceServiceRequest dict by iterating resourceSpans→scopeSpans→spans and merging resource attributes down.
preconditions: otlp_request is a valid parsed JSON dict
postconditions: Returns a list of OtlpSpan, one per span found in the request
Each OtlpSpan inherits resource_attributes from its parent resourceSpan
Invalid individual spans are skipped with a logged warning

structured_side_effects.description: Logs warnings for malformed individual spans that are skipped

functions.name: OtlpSource._span_to_event
functions.description: Pure conversion function: transforms an OtlpSpan into an Event. Generates a UUID4 event_id, converts start_time_unix_nano to ISO 8601 UTC timestamp, sets event_kind to 'otlp.trace.{normalized_span_name}', and packs span attributes into payload. Sets trace_id and span_id as correlation_keys.

inputs.name: source_name
inputs.description: Source identifier for the Event.source field

postconditions: Returned Event has a fresh UUID4 event_id
Event.event_kind starts with 'otlp.trace.'
Event.timestamp is derived from span.start_time_unix_nano in ISO 8601 UTC
Event.correlation_keys contains trace_id and span_id
Event.source equals source_name parameter

functions.name: FileSource.subscribe
functions.description: Register an async event handler callback for file-tailed events. Synchronous operation.
preconditions: callback is a non-null async callable
postconditions: callback is appended to internal subscriber list

structured_side_effects.description: Appends callback to internal subscriber list

functions.name: FileSource.start
functions.description: Begin the asyncio polling loop. Records initial file offset (end of file if file exists, 0 if not yet created). Uses asyncio.Event for shutdown signaling. The polling loop runs as an asyncio Task.
preconditions: Source is in CREATED state (not already started)
postconditions: Polling asyncio.Task is created and running
Source state transitions to RUNNING
If file exists, initial offset is set to end of file (tail mode)
If file does not exist, polling waits for file creation

structured_side_effects.description: Stats file to determine initial offset
Transitions source state from CREATED to RUNNING
Logs file path and initial offset

functions.name: FileSource.stop
functions.description: Signal the polling loop to stop via asyncio.Event, await task completion. Idempotent.
postconditions: Polling task has completed
Source state transitions to STOPPED
asyncio.Event is set (shutdown signal)

structured_side_effects.description: Transitions source state to STOPPED
Logs shutdown

functions.name: FileSource._poll_loop
functions.description: Internal async polling loop. On each iteration: stat file to detect truncation or rotation (inode change), seek to tracked offset, read new data via aiofiles, split into lines buffering partial lines, parse each complete line as JSON then validate against Event schema, emit valid events. Sleeps for poll_interval_seconds between iterations. Loop exits when shutdown asyncio.Event is set.

error_cases.name: file_not_found
error_cases.condition: File does not exist on poll iteration

error_data.message: File not found, will retry next poll

error_cases.name: permission_denied
error_cases.condition: File exists but is not readable

error_data.message: Permission denied reading file, will retry

error_cases.name: invalid_json_line
error_cases.condition: A line is not valid JSON

error_data.message: Skipping invalid JSON line at offset {offset}

error_cases.name: invalid_event_line
error_cases.condition: JSON is valid but does not match Event schema

preconditions: Called internally by start(), shutdown_event is not set
postconditions: All complete JSONL lines since last poll are parsed and emitted as Events
FilePosition.offset is updated to current read position
Partial lines are buffered in FilePosition.line_buffer
File truncation resets offset to 0
File rotation (inode change) resets offset to 0 and clears line_buffer

structured_side_effects.description: Reads new lines from tracked offset via aiofiles
Updates file offset, inode, and line buffer
Logs per-line parse errors, file not found, permission errors, truncation/rotation detection

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n = new lines since last poll

functions.name: SentinelSource.subscribe
functions.description: Register an async event handler callback for Sentinel-derived events. Synchronous operation.
preconditions: callback is a non-null async callable
postconditions: callback is appended to internal subscriber list

structured_side_effects.description: Appends callback to internal subscriber list

functions.name: SentinelSource.start
functions.description: Initialize aiohttp AppRunner and TCPSite (reusing webhook HTTP transport pattern), bind to configured host:port, begin accepting Sentinel incident lifecycle webhook POSTs. Sentinel-specific JSON parsing extracts incident fields and applies event_kind_prefix per AS9.

error_cases.name: bind_failed
error_cases.condition: Configured host:port is unavailable or in use

preconditions: Source is in CREATED state (not already started)
postconditions: HTTP server is listening on configured bind address and port
Source state transitions to RUNNING

structured_side_effects.description: Creates aiohttp AppRunner and TCPSite for Sentinel webhooks
Transitions source state from CREATED to RUNNING
Logs successful bind

functions.name: SentinelSource.stop
functions.description: Gracefully shut down the Sentinel HTTP server. Idempotent.
postconditions: HTTP server is no longer accepting connections
Source state transitions to STOPPED

structured_side_effects.description: Shuts down aiohttp AppRunner and TCPSite
Transitions source state to STOPPED
Logs shutdown

functions.name: SentinelSource._handle_post
functions.description: Internal HTTP POST handler for Sentinel webhooks. Parses Sentinel-specific JSON structure (incident properties, severity, status, etc.), constructs Event with event_kind = '{event_kind_prefix}.incident.{action}' (e.g. 'sentinel.incident.created'), generates UUID4 event_id, extracts incident_id and incident_number as correlation_keys. All exceptions caught internally.

inputs.name: request_body
inputs.description: Raw HTTP request body containing Sentinel webhook JSON

error_cases.name: unrecognized_payload
error_cases.condition: JSON does not contain expected Sentinel incident structure

postconditions: Returns status 200 on successful parse and emit
Returns status 400 on invalid JSON or unrecognized Sentinel payload
Returns status 415 if Content-Type is not application/json
Event.event_kind starts with configured event_kind_prefix followed by '.incident.'
Event.correlation_keys includes Sentinel incident_id if present

structured_side_effects.description: Logs parse errors and unexpected exceptions with request context

invariants: All sources implement SourceProtocol (start, stop, subscribe) and are runtime_checkable
Sources never raise exceptions to callers during event processing — all exceptions are caught, logged, and processing continues (AC13)
HTTP sources (webhook, otlp, sentinel) validate Content-Type header before parsing body
HTTP sources enforce client_max_size_bytes on request bodies
The emit() utility calls every subscriber even if earlier subscribers raise exceptions
FileSource handles file truncation by resetting offset to 0
FileSource handles file rotation (inode change) by resetting offset to 0 and clearing line buffer
FileSource buffers partial lines and only emits complete JSONL lines
SentinelSource prefixes all event_kind values with the configured event_kind_prefix (AS9)
OtlpSource flattens resourceSpans→scopeSpans→spans into individual Events
OtlpSource returns empty JSON object '{}' per OTLP HTTP specification
start() raises RuntimeError if source is already in RUNNING state
stop() is idempotent — safe to call multiple times or on never-started sources
subscribe() may be called before or after start()
All Events emitted by sources have a valid UUID4 event_id and ISO 8601 UTC timestamp
Source state transitions follow CREATED→RUNNING→STOPPED (no reverse transitions)

data_access.rationale: Sources read INTERNAL-tier data: webhook and sentinel sources receive event payloads via HTTP POST from internal services within the governance stack; OTLP source receives trace spans from instrumented applications; file source reads JSONL event files from the local filesystem written by other components. No PII is expected in the event envelope fields (event_id, event_kind, timestamp, source, correlation_keys), though payload dicts may contain arbitrary INTERNAL operational data. Sources do not write to any persistent store — they only emit events to in-memory subscriber callbacks.

side_effects.rationale: Webhook, OTLP, and Sentinel sources bind TCP ports and accept HTTP POST requests containing event data from internal governance stack services
File source reads JSONL lines from local filesystem using aiofiles, tracking read position for resumption
All sources invoke registered subscriber callbacks with parsed Event objects for downstream processing by the correlation engine
